itemLeads.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, January 21, 1991
itemLeads.scopeAndContentNote: In a busy day at the White House, President Bush met with his senior national security and defense advisors about ongoing hostilities in the Persian Gulf. He also briefed members of Congress, including House Speaker Thomas Foley of Washington. Later in the day, he met with the former Prime Minister of Singapore. Throughout the day, the President made numerous telephone calls to world leaders, including the Prime Ministers of Spain, Italy, and Turkey.

itemLeads.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, January 30, 1991
itemLeads.scopeAndContentNote: The President met with his senior staff for his morning national intelligence and security briefings. Following these meetings, the President had his morning meeting with Chief of Staff John Sununu and telephoned Turkish President Turgat Ozal. He then met with Antonios Samaras, Foreign Minister of Greece. Later in the morning, he traveled to Capitol Hill where he attended the 50th anniversary of President Franklin D. Roosevelt's Four Freedoms Speech. He also met with Congressional leadership including Senator Robert Dole and Congressman Bob Michel. In the evening, the President dined with the Reverend Billy Graham.

itemLeads.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, February 5, 1991
itemLeads.scopeAndContentNote: Prior to his morning national security and intelligence briefings, the President played tennis with Chris Evert. Later in the morning, he spoke by telephone with President Carlos Salinas of Mexico, Prime Minister Brian Mulroney of Canada, and President Turgat Ozal of Turkey. Meetings with his domestic policy staff including Roger B. Porter followed. Before lunch, the President held his 71st news conference for members of the press. In the afternoon, the President spoke with President Francois Mitterand of France, and met with senior advisors to discuss the Highway Reauthorization Bill. In the evening after dinner, President Bush spoke with Margaret Thatcher and Walter H. Annenberg.

itemLeads.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, February 22, 1991
itemLeads.scopeAndContentNote: The President received his morning intelligence and national security briefings. Later in the morning, the President spoke with Francois Mitterand, President of France and met with a delegation from Turkey. He spoke by conference call with Hosni Mubarak, President of Egypt, and then released a statement to the press regarding Operation Desert Storm. After swearing in Lynn Martin as Secretary of Labor, the President spoke on a conference call with Mikhail Gorbachev. He also held a meeting with Richard B. Cheney and Colin L. Powell to discuss Operation Desert Storm.

recordsWithReferences.title: Telephone Conversation with President Turgut Ozal of Turkey, January 21, 1991

references.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, January 21, 1991
references.scopeAndContentNote: In a busy day at the White House, President Bush met with his senior national security and defense advisors about ongoing hostilities in the Persian Gulf. He also briefed members of Congress, including House Speaker Thomas Foley of Washington. Later in the day, he met with the former Prime Minister of Singapore. Throughout the day, the President made numerous telephone calls to world leaders, including the Prime Ministers of Spain, Italy, and Turkey.

recordsWithReferences.title: Telephone Conversation with President Turgut Ozal of Turkey, January 30, 1991

references.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, January 30, 1991
references.scopeAndContentNote: The President met with his senior staff for his morning national intelligence and security briefings. Following these meetings, the President had his morning meeting with Chief of Staff John Sununu and telephoned Turkish President Turgat Ozal. He then met with Antonios Samaras, Foreign Minister of Greece. Later in the morning, he traveled to Capitol Hill where he attended the 50th anniversary of President Franklin D. Roosevelt's Four Freedoms Speech. He also met with Congressional leadership including Senator Robert Dole and Congressman Bob Michel. In the evening, the President dined with the Reverend Billy Graham.

recordsWithReferences.title: Telephone Conversation with President Turgut Ozal of Turkey, February 5, 1991

references.title: President's Daily Diary Entry, February 5, 1991
references.scopeAndContentNote: Prior to his morning national security and intelligence briefings, the President played tennis with Chris Evert. Later in the morning, he spoke by telephone with President Carlos Salinas of Mexico, Prime Minister Brian Mulroney of Canada, and President Turgat Ozal of Turkey. Meetings with his domestic policy staff including Roger B. Porter followed. Before lunch, the President held his 71st news conference for members of the press. In the afternoon, the President spoke with President Francois Mitterand of France, and met with senior advisors to discuss the Highway Reauthorization Bill. In the evening after dinner, President Bush spoke with Margaret Thatcher and Walter H. Annenberg.

recordsWithReferences.title: Telephone Conversation with President Turgut Ozal of Turkey, February 15, 1991

references.title: [Presidential Daily Diary] 2/15/1991
references.scopeAndContentNote: In the morning, the President received his national security and intelligence briefings. He then met with members of the American Association for the Advancement of Science. In the late morning, the President traveled aboard Air Force One to Massachusetts and then spoke to employees of the Raytheon Plant, makers of the Patriot Missile. He then participated in a Patriot Missile simulation. From there, the President traveled to Kennebunkport, Maine.

recordsWithReferences.title: Telcon with President Turgut Ozal of Turkey on February 19, 1991

references.title: [Presidential Daily Diary] 2/19/1991
references.scopeAndContentNote: Back at the White House after a weekend at Kennebunkport, the President met with his national security and intelligence advisors. He then met with his senior advisors to discuss Operation Desert Storm. Before lunch, he participated in a photo opportunity with members of the American Association of Engineering Societies. He also held a meeting with his domestic policy staff including Roger B. Porter and Lamar Alexander, Secretary of Education. Following lunch, the President spoke on a conference call with Francois Mitterand, President of France and met with Richard G. Darman, Director of the Office of Management and Budget. He then met with the Secretary of the Interior Manuel Lujan, Jr., to discuss the opening of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.

recordsWithReferences.title: Telcon with Turgut Ozal, President of Turkey on February 26, 1991

references.title: [Presidential Daily Diary] 2/26/1991
references.scopeAndContentNote: With the Persian Gulf War nearing an end, the President continued to speak daily with heads of state around the world. He began this day with a conversation by telephone with Vaclav Havel, President of Czechoslovakia. He then had his morning national security and intelligence briefings and met with senior advisors to discuss Operation Desert Storm. At 9:48 a.m., the President addressed the nation to announce Iraq's withdrawal from Kuwait. Following these remarks, members of Congress were briefed by the President on fast-track authority. Prior to lunch, he met with Cesar Gaviria Trujillo, President of Columbia. The afternoon consisted of additional meetings on Operation Desert Storm with Colin L. Powell and Richard B. Cheney in attendence.

sourcePool.label: Presidential Daily Diary and Backup
sourcePool.title: Presidential Daily Diary and Presidential Daily Backup Materials
sourcePool.scopeAndContentNote:
The daily diary and daily backup chronicle the daily activities of the President, including personal and private meetings (with lists of attendees), events, social and speaking engagements, trips, telephone calls, meals, routine tasks, and recreational pursuits.  The daily diary and the daily backup do not contain summaries of telephone calls or meeting minutes.

For any given meeting, telephone call, or event, the daily diary usually lists the time, location, persons involved (or a reference to an appendix listing individuals present), and, in cases where ambiguity prevails, a brief description of what took place.  Except in rare instances, each daily diary entry provides a comprehensive list of presidential activities.  However, there are missing dates (sometimes covering as long as a month) in the series.  They generally occurred when the President was on vacation, most often in August and December.  These omissions are clearly marked in the folder title list.

The daily backup contains telephone memoranda that record times of calls, status (incoming or outgoing), and parties involved.  Other materials in the daily backup include briefing memoranda used to inform the President about the purpose, duration, and size of upcoming events; schedules describing the minute-by-minute visits of foreign leaders; documentation supporting trips and short excursions of the President; meeting agendas and talking points (in the form of outlines) for some cabinet meetings and diplomatic conferences; notes concerning recommended telephone calls for the 1992 Presidential Campaign; and items concerning President Bush's weekend stays at Camp David.

The diary entries from January 15, 1991 to February 27, 1991 cover the period of Operation Desert Storm, otherwise known as the Persian Gulf War.  The entries document the President's daily intelligence and national security briefings and his frequent contacts with the heads of state of coalition partners.
sourcePool.note: User-requested Presidential Daily Diary and Daily Backup source for meetings, calls, schedules, attendees, and call-status corroboration.
